entries.title: Emblemata Sacra - Heart Emblem (plate, seq. 21)
entries.creator: Daniel Cramer
entries.summary:
Plate from Daniel Cramer's *Emblemata Sacra*: a circular emblem of the human heart pierced by an arrow or sword, the recurring devotional-alchemical image at the centre of the book.

## Iconography
A round emblematic medallion frames a single great heart pierced by an arrow or sword, set in a small landscape and ringed by a Latin motto; the page is one half of an opening that pairs the engraving with a Latin and German verse and a scriptural citation. The specific motto and biblical text of this individual plate are not identified here, so only the shared visual programme of the series is described, to avoid invention.

## Significance
Cramer's *Emblemata Sacra* (Frankfurt, 1624) is a cycle of devotional heart-emblems in which a single heart is subjected, plate by plate, to a sequence of operations - wounded, burned, watered, washed, hammered, weighed, winged and finally crowned. The imagery deliberately borrows the language of the alchemical laboratory: the heart is treated like *prima materia* purified in the furnace, so that the believer's inner transformation mirrors the refining of metals. Here this plate shows the wounding of the heart that opens it to grace - the *cor vulneratum* of Passion devotion. The flaming, operated heart made Cramer's emblems enormously popular and they were widely reused across 17th-century devotional and esoteric print, carrying a Rosicrucian resonance into Protestant piety. The motto-image-verse-citation structure is the classic tripartite emblem form codified by Andrea Alciato.

## For artists & game designers
The 'one heart, many operations' series is a ready template for a transformation track: a single object (heart, gem, core) shown wounded -> fired -> washed -> winged -> crowned across stages, each stage a discrete upgrade icon.

The imagery deliberately borrows the language of the alchemical laboratory: the heart is treated like *prima materia* purified in the furnace, so that the believer's inner transformation mirrors the refining of metals. Here this plate shows the heart beaten and shaped under the hammer, like base metal worked toward perfection. The flaming, operated heart made Cramer's emblems enormously popular and they were widely reused across 17th-century devotional and esoteric print, carrying a Rosicrucian resonance into Protestant piety. The motto-image-verse-citation structure is the classic tripartite emblem form codified by Andrea Alciato.

## For artists & game designers
The 'one heart, many operations' series is a ready template for a transformation track: a single object (heart, gem, core) shown wounded -> fired -> washed -> winged -> crowned across stages, each stage a discrete upgrade icon.
